hawkish_phrases: second-round effects will prove more enduring in the medium term
underlying domestic inflationary pressures appeared more entrenched
Monetary policy will need to continue to remain restrictive for sufficiently long
services consumer price inflation has declined to 5.7% in June
pay growth had remained in excess of rates explained by Bank models
dovish_phrases: It is now appropriate to reduce slightly the degree of policy restrictiveness
The impact from past external shocks has abated
restrictive stance of monetary policy continues to weigh on activity in the real economy
Domestic inflationary persistence is expected to fade away over the next few years
The Committee expected the fall in headline inflation, and normalisation in many indicators of inflation expectations, to continue to feed through to weaker pay and price-setting dynamics
summary: The MPC voted 5-4 to cut Bank Rate by 25bp to 5%, with the majority judging it appropriate to reduce slightly the degree of policy restrictiveness despite meaningful hawkish dissent citing entrenched domestic inflationary pressures and stronger-than-expected GDP.
